problem_summary: The database already accepts entry_type=thought, but the active Skill, preview protocol, search surface, weekly review, and README do not make thought a usable first-class record type. Ordinary captures currently default to diary without an input-level classification that the user can inspect and correct. The prior Project_PA database also contains nine confirmed thought inputs for the real user that were intentionally omitted from the earlier diary-only migration.
impact: Users cannot reliably distinguish conceptual ideas from event-and-reflection journals, cannot filter recall by that distinction, and weekly review can mix the two without labeling. Nine confirmed historical thoughts remain outside the current local-first archive. The change will make the type explicit and auditable while keeping physics, biology, life, philosophy, and similar subjects in the existing theme/tag layer.
proposed_diff:
1. Update the active record-life-journal Skill metadata and capture protocol to treat ordinary recordable input as one indivisible unit whose entry type is exactly diary or thought. Define diary as an input primarily anchored to lived events, feelings, personal status, or time-specific reflection; define thought as an input primarily intended to preserve a proposition, hypothesis, conceptual question, model, interpretation, or reusable insight. Explicit user type wins. Mixed content receives one dominant input-level type and is never split into different entry types; ordered segments still support multiple themes. Direct informational questions remain conversation unless the user asks to record them. Decision and weekly keep their existing special workflows.
2. Add an entry-type classification contract to agent-protocol.md and require every ordinary preview to display the proposed diary/thought type. Add an optional save-preview entry-type argument that may switch only non-confirmed ordinary entries between diary and thought; reject attempts to convert decision/weekly records or overwrite confirmed entries. Persist the chosen type in SQLite and Markdown frontmatter.
3. Extend local recall/search with an optional entry-type filter and expose weekly diary_entries and thought_entries separately while retaining the existing combined entries field for compatibility. Update weekly instructions to summarize events/reflections separately from ideas, without imposing a thought-specific schema or external API.
4. Add unit tests for whole-input exclusivity, preview correction, invalid special-type conversion, Markdown persistence, type-filtered search, separated weekly context, and backward compatibility.
5. After implementation validation, read Project_PA/data/project_pa.db in read-only mode and prepare an audited migration preview for exactly the nine nonempty, user-confirmed rows matching user_id=2 and lower(category)=thought. Exclude testuser user_id=1 and all other categories. Preserve each source row as one thought input, its verbatim content, source date, UUID/topic metadata in the manifest, and a unique source key project_pa:entries:<id>@<timestamp>. Check source-key and content-hash duplicates against the target. Use the normal create-draft, save-preview, and explicit confirmation boundary; never modify the source database. Create data/imports/project_pa_thoughts.json and project_pa_thoughts_preview.md with source/target hashes, themes/tags, entry ids, and paths. Back up the target database before applying.
6. Expand the repository README with user-facing thought examples, diary-versus-thought boundaries, the rule that one input has one type, subject themes/tags, preview correction, type-filtered recall, weekly separation, CLI/API details, storage behavior, and the completed Project_PA thought migration audit/result.
compatibility: No database schema expansion is required because thought is already an allowed entry_type. Existing diary, weekly, and decision records remain unchanged. Existing create-draft and search calls keep their defaults; new type arguments are optional. weekly-context retains the combined entries output while adding separated lists. The migration is additive, deduplicated by source identity/content hash, excludes the Project_PA test user, and leaves the source database untouched.
tests: SQLite PRAGMA integrity_check and foreign_key_check on data/diary.sqlite3
Read-only Project_PA candidate audit: exactly 9 confirmed user_id=2 thought rows, 0 testuser rows, stable source hashes
Post-migration audit: 9 new confirmed thought entries, unique source keys, verbatim originals and cleaned Markdown present, FTS rows present, no duplicate prior diary imports
token_cost_change: neutral to slight increase: one compact entry-type classification and visible preview label are added to ordinary capture; type filtering reduces irrelevant recall, and no mandatory thought-analysis template or external model call is introduced
